text:
Once upon a time in a cozy little cottage at the edge of a lush green forest, there lived a wise old cat named Whiskers. Whiskers was not just any cat; she was known throughout the land for her impeccable sense of direction and her uncanny ability to foresee the weather.

One sunny morning, as the first rays of sunlight peeked through the window, Whiskers noticed something unusual. The air felt heavy with moisture, and there was an inexplicable stillness that pervaded the air. She decided it was high time to use her special skills and went to visit her friend, Mr. Tiddles, the local rabbit.

"Mr. Tiddles," said Whiskers, her voice gentle but firm, "I have a feeling we might see a storm later today."

Mr. Tiddles hopped closer, ears perked up. "Really, Whiskers? How do you know?"

Whiskers purred softly, her eyes gleaming with confidence.
